purpose: Define a private, explicit Operator-principal assignment substrate for workspace-scoped future policy evaluation without deriving authority from authentication, membership, ownership, browser input, repository state, or environment state.

owning_architecture.immediate_product_owner: §1 / Facade protected workspace and authentication architecture

identity_source: auth.uid() only; caller-supplied user identifiers are not trusted

uniqueness_and_reprovisioning_rules.non_revoked_unique_assignment: At most one active or suspended assignment exists for the same workspace_id and user_id.

provisioning_provenance.provisioned_by_reference: required nonempty administrative executor reference; not an auth.users identity placeholder
provisioning_provenance.provisioning_authorization_reference: required nonempty external authorization reference for administrative provisioning only
provisioning_provenance.provisioning_execution_reference: required nonempty execution reference for the administrative SQL operation
forbidden_material: invented authenticated-user IDs representing the SQL administrator

private_schema_posture.existing_schema_conflict_behavior: fail migration application if a pre-existing private schema is not owned by postgres

no_client_access_rule: Browser-facing roles receive no schema usage, table privileges, permissive RLS policy, resolver execute grant, public wrapper, general RPC, or membership-based access path.
bootstrap_rule: The migration creates zero positive Operator-principal rows; the first assignment remains a separately authorized administrative SQL operation.
static_validation_boundary: The repository validator inspects only explicit tracked paths and reports static structure evidence, not runtime database proof.
executable_validation_boundary: Executable database validation is authorized only through an existing repository-supported ephemeral harness; absent that harness, behavior remains not executably verified locally.
live_application_boundary: No live Supabase migration application, project write, Operator provisioning, authorization issuance, approval, witness, replay state, deployment, or push is part of this phase.
future_palisade_relationship: Palisade may later consume the private resolved Operator principal as one input after a separately authorized access boundary exists; this phase does not change Palisade policy or runtime behavior.
future_conduit_relationship: Conduit may later transport authorization flows after separate authorization; this phase does not change Conduit behavior.
